mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth signals expanding capacity and economic output, which drives higher corporate earnings and stock prices over the subsequent month. If INDPRO has risen for three consecutive months, this always-on trend predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production leads corporate profits in historical data
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03 or positive forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of trailing 12-month windows when INDPRO 3-month growth is above 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, further increases indicate severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent week. The signal activates when UNRATE crosses above 6%.
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment above 6% historically precedes bear markets
failure_condition: In any 2-year window when UNRATE > 6%, the median 5-day forward return is not negative or the Sharpe ratio of this signal is below -0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand and residential investment, which jointly drive economic momentum and corporate profitability. This interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Both series are coincident indicators of domestic demand
failure_condition: When both RSAFS 3-month growth and HOUST 3-month growth exceed 2%, the 60-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of cases over a 5-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 5.5%), subsequent declines in the unemployment rate signal improving labor market conditions that fuel consumer spending and equity gains. The signal is especially potent when UNRATE is elevated.
evidence_refs: fact:Declining unemployment from high levels historically accompanies early cycle stock rallies
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.5% and decreases month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day return is negative more than 50% of the time over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises above 3% year-over-year, the central bank is expected to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations. This threshold trigger predicts negative short-term stock returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation above 3% has historically led to Fed tightening and market selloffs
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 3%, the 5-day forward return is positive in at least 60% of instances over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth signals the overall health of the economy and corporate earnings; positive quarterly GDP growth consistently supports equity prices. This always-on trend signal uses year-over-year GDP expansion to predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is the broadest measure of economic activity and correlates with earnings
failure_condition: If GDPC1 year-over-year growth is positive, but 60-day forward returns are negative more than 40% of the time in a 10-year sample, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising housing starts signal accommodative monetary policy fueling a housing recovery, which amplifies economic growth and equity returns. The joint condition is more predictive than either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:Low rates and housing strength characterized the post-2009 recovery
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing recovery
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2% and HOUST 3-month growth exceeds 5%, the 20-day forward return is positive in less than 55% of cases over a 5-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary environment where industrial production is declining year-over-year, a positive surprise in payroll growth signals early economic recovery and a turning point, generating strong positive equity returns. The interaction of declining INDPRO and rising PAYEMS is regime-specific.
evidence_refs: fact:Early employment gains during recessions precede market bottoms
failure_condition: When INDPRO YoY is negative and PAYEMS month-over-month change is positive, the 20-day forward return is positive in fewer than 60% of instances over any 2-year recessionary window.
